title: Fallers
description: Use axes or chainsaws to fell trees using knowledge of tree characteristics and cutting techniques to control direction of fall and minimize tree damage.

tasks.text: Stop saw engines, pull cutting bars from cuts, and run to safety as tree falls.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Appraise trees for certain characteristics, such as twist, rot, and heavy limb growth, and gauge amount and direction of lean, to determine how to control the direction of a tree's fall with the least damage.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Saw back-cuts, leaving sufficient sound wood to control direction of fall.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Clear brush from work areas and escape routes, and cut saplings and other trees from direction of falls, using axes, chainsaws, or bulldozers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure felled trees and cut them into specified log lengths, using chain saws and axes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assess logs after cutting to ensure that the quality and length are correct.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Determine position, direction, and depth of cuts to be made, and placement of wedges or jacks.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Control the direction of a tree's fall by scoring cutting lines with axes, sawing undercuts along scored lines with chainsaws, knocking slabs from cuts with single-bit axes, and driving wedges.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Trim off the tops and limbs of trees, using chainsaws, delimbers, or axes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select trees to be cut down, assessing factors such as site, terrain, and weather conditions before beginning work.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Maintain and repair chainsaws and other equipment, cleaning, oiling, and greasing equipment, and sharpening equipment properly.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Insert jacks or drive wedges behind saws to prevent binding of saws and to start trees falling.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Tag unsafe trees with high-visibility ribbons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Secure steel cables or chains to logs for dragging by tractors or for pulling by cable yarding systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Load logs or wood onto trucks, trailers, or railroad cars, by hand or using loaders or winches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Work as a member of a team, rotating between chain saw operation and skidder operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place supporting limbs or poles under felled trees to avoid splitting undersides, and to prevent logs from rolling.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
